content.text:
{
  "dimension": "intervention-quality",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "Every move is matched to what was just noticed and lands at the moment the client can receive it. Turn 2 names the read (intellectual question vs. emotional body) and pivots with an invitation rather than an answer. Turn 4 deploys the 'most profound positive shift' move to surface the real want. Turns 10-14 use the 'what's essentially you?' move to point at awareness as substrate before addressing the parts construct. Turn 16 dissolves identification with the framework without devaluing it by offering alternative framings. Turn 18 names the emerging insight precisely. Turn 22 is the signature inversion ('how do you NOT trust it?') deployed at the exact moment the client locks into a 'how do I' frame. Turns 24-32 use the live present-moment evidence ('you just spoke') to demonstrate that the supposedly-impossible thing is already happening — this is earned, not prescribed. Turn 34 closes the loop by dissolving the original question. Turn 38 applies the principle ('if you think you're making a decision, you're in fear') and Turn 40 grounds it with concrete micro-examples. Sequence integrity is intact: read → want → awareness → dissolution → principle → grounding. Coach holds moments (short utterances in 6, 10, 26, 28, 30) and lets the client complete the insight rather than rushing.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 10, 12, 16, 18, 22, 26, 30, 32, 34, 38, 40],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the read and pivots with an invitation rather than answering the intellectual question."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Deploys 'most profound positive shift' to surface the real want under the rehearsed question."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Minimal acknowledgment — doesn't over-process the want, just confirms and moves."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'What's you?' — points at awareness-as-substrate at the right moment after parts work is described."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Follows up when first attempt doesn't fully land; pivots angle rather than repeating."},
    {"turn": 16, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Dissolves identification with the parts framework by offering alternative framings; doesn't devalue it."},
    {"turn": 18, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the bigger insight precisely at the moment client is ready to hear it."},
    {"turn": 20, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'What's the problem with that?' — invites the client to surface the actual stuck point."},
    {"turn": 22, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Signature inversion 'how do you NOT trust it?' deployed exactly when client locks into 'how do I' frame."},
    {"turn": 24, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Pivots to present-moment evidence when the inversion doesn't fully land."},
    {"turn": 26, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Holds the moment, lets the simple observation land."},
    {"turn": 30, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Persists with present-moment pointing rather than abandoning when client intellectualizes."},
    {"turn": 32, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Synthesis move integrating parts + awareness + present-moment evidence."},
    {"turn": 34, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Closes the loop — dissolves the original question by showing it's already answered."},
    {"turn": 38, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Principle delivered at the moment client articulates the decision-vs-flow duality."},
    {"turn": 40, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Grounds the abstract principle with concrete micro-action examples."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["follow-wonder-flag-the-deflection", "press-the-button-thought-experiment", "find-the-want-behind-the-should", "idea-of-self-vs-actual-self", "how-do-you-not-trust-it", "whats-the-most-profound-positive-shift", "what-am-i"],
  "expected_territory_misses": [],
  "notes": "Textbook example of move-to-read matching with proper sequencing. The 'you just spoke' sequence is the unit of effective coaching — present-moment evidence used to dissolve an abstract question."
}
